description: This domain emulates different environments for the page.

properties.name: top
properties.description: Overrides safe-area-inset-top.

properties.name: topMax
properties.description: Overrides safe-area-max-inset-top.

properties.name: left
properties.description: Overrides safe-area-inset-left.

properties.name: leftMax
properties.description: Overrides safe-area-max-inset-left.

properties.name: bottom
properties.description: Overrides safe-area-inset-bottom.

properties.name: bottomMax
properties.description: Overrides safe-area-max-inset-bottom.

properties.name: right
properties.description: Overrides safe-area-inset-right.

properties.name: rightMax
properties.description: Overrides safe-area-max-inset-right.

properties.name: type
properties.description: Orientation type.

properties.name: angle
properties.description: Orientation angle.

properties.name: orientation
properties.description: Orientation of a display feature in relation to screen

properties.name: offset
properties.description:
The offset from the screen origin in either the x (for vertical
orientation) or y (for horizontal orientation) direction.

properties.name: maskLength
properties.description:
A display feature may mask content such that it is not physically
displayed - this length along with the offset describes this area.
A display feature that only splits content will have a 0 mask_length.

properties.name: type
properties.description: Current posture of the device

types.description:
advance: If the scheduler runs out of immediate work, the virtual time base may fast forward to
allow the next delayed task (if any) to run; pause: The virtual time base may not advance;
pauseIfNetworkFetchesPending: The virtual time base may not advance if there are any pending
resource fetches.
Used to specify User Agent Client Hints to emulate. See https://wicg.github.io/ua-client-hints

Used to specify User Agent Client Hints to emulate. See https://wicg.github.io/ua-client-hints
Missing optional values will be filled in by the target with what it would normally use.

properties.name: brands
properties.description: Brands appearing in Sec-CH-UA.

properties.name: fullVersionList
properties.description: Brands appearing in Sec-CH-UA-Full-Version-List.

properties.name: formFactors
properties.description:
Used to specify User Agent form-factor values.
See https://wicg.github.io/ua-client-hints/#sec-ch-ua-form-factors

types.description:
Used to specify sensor types to emulate.
See https://w3c.github.io/sensors/#automation for more information.

properties.name: top
properties.description: Work area top inset in pixels. Default is 0;

properties.name: left
properties.description: Work area left inset in pixels. Default is 0;

properties.name: bottom
properties.description: Work area bottom inset in pixels. Default is 0;

properties.name: right
properties.description: Work area right inset in pixels. Default is 0;

types.description:
Screen information similar to the one returned by window.getScreenDetails() method,
see https://w3c.github.io/window-management/#screendetailed.

properties.name: left
properties.description: Offset of the left edge of the screen.

properties.name: top
properties.description: Offset of the top edge of the screen.

properties.name: width
properties.description: Width of the screen.

properties.name: height
properties.description: Height of the screen.

properties.name: availLeft
properties.description: Offset of the left edge of the available screen area.

properties.name: availTop
properties.description: Offset of the top edge of the available screen area.

properties.name: availWidth
properties.description: Width of the available screen area.

properties.name: availHeight
properties.description: Height of the available screen area.

properties.name: devicePixelRatio
properties.description: Specifies the screen's device pixel ratio.

properties.name: orientation
properties.description: Specifies the screen's orientation.

properties.name: colorDepth
properties.description: Specifies the screen's color depth in bits.

properties.name: isExtended
properties.description: Indicates whether the device has multiple screens.

properties.name: isInternal
properties.description: Indicates whether the screen is internal to the device or external, attached to the device.

properties.name: isPrimary
properties.description: Indicates whether the screen is set as the the operating system primary screen.

properties.name: label
properties.description: Specifies the descriptive label for the screen.

properties.name: id
properties.description: Specifies the unique identifier of the screen.

types.description: Enum of image types that can be disabled.

commands.name: canEmulate
commands.description: Tells whether emulation is supported.

commands.name: clearDeviceMetricsOverride
commands.description: Clears the overridden device metrics.

commands.name: clearGeolocationOverride
commands.description: Clears the overridden Geolocation Position and Error.

commands.name: resetPageScaleFactor
commands.description: Requests that page scale factor is reset to initial values.

commands.name: setFocusEmulationEnabled
commands.description: Enables or disables simulating a focused and active page.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Whether to enable to disable focus emulation.

commands.name: setAutoDarkModeOverride
commands.description: Automatically render all web contents using a dark theme.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description:
Whether to enable or disable automatic dark mode.
If not specified, any existing override will be cleared.

commands.name: setCPUThrottlingRate
commands.description: Enables CPU throttling to emulate slow CPUs.

parameters.name: rate
parameters.description: Throttling rate as a slowdown factor (1 is no throttle, 2 is 2x slowdown, etc).

commands.name: setDefaultBackgroundColorOverride
commands.description:
Sets or clears an override of the default background color of the frame. This override is used
if the content does not specify one.

parameters.name: color
parameters.description:
RGBA of the default background color. If not specified, any existing override will be
cleared.

commands.name: setSafeAreaInsetsOverride
commands.description:
Overrides the values for env(safe-area-inset-*) and env(safe-area-max-inset-*). Unset values will cause the
respective variables to be undefined, even if previously overridden.

commands.name: setDeviceMetricsOverride
commands.description:
Overrides the values of device screen dimensions (window.screen.width, window.screen.height,
window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight, and "device-width"/"device-height"-related CSS media
query results).

parameters.name: width
parameters.description: Overriding width value in pixels (minimum 0, maximum 10000000). 0 disables the override.

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: Overriding height value in pixels (minimum 0, maximum 10000000). 0 disables the override.

parameters.name: deviceScaleFactor
parameters.description: Overriding device scale factor value. 0 disables the override.

parameters.name: mobile
parameters.description:
Whether to emulate mobile device. This includes viewport meta tag, overlay scrollbars, text
autosizing and more.

parameters.name: scale
parameters.description: Scale to apply to resulting view image.

parameters.name: screenWidth
parameters.description: Overriding screen width value in pixels (minimum 0, maximum 10000000).

parameters.name: screenHeight
parameters.description: Overriding screen height value in pixels (minimum 0, maximum 10000000).

parameters.name: positionX
parameters.description: Overriding view X position on screen in pixels (minimum 0, maximum 10000000).

parameters.name: positionY
parameters.description: Overriding view Y position on screen in pixels (minimum 0, maximum 10000000).

parameters.name: dontSetVisibleSize
parameters.description: Do not set visible view size, rely upon explicit setVisibleSize call.

parameters.name: screenOrientation
parameters.description: Screen orientation override.

parameters.name: viewport
parameters.description:
If set, the visible area of the page will be overridden to this viewport. This viewport
change is not observed by the page, e.g. viewport-relative elements do not change positions.

parameters.name: displayFeature
parameters.description:
If set, the display feature of a multi-segment screen. If not set, multi-segment support
is turned-off.
Deprecated, use Emulation.setDisplayFeaturesOverride.

parameters.name: devicePosture
parameters.description:
If set, the posture of a foldable device. If not set the posture is set
to continuous.
Deprecated, use Emulation.setDevicePostureOverride.

parameters.name: scrollbarType
parameters.description: Scrollbar type. Default: `default`.

parameters.name: screenOrientationLockEmulation
parameters.description:
If set to true, enables screen orientation lock emulation, which
intercepts screen.orientation.lock() calls from the page and reports
orientation changes via screenOrientationLockChanged events. This is
useful for emulating mobile device orientation lock behavior in
responsive design mode.

commands.name: setDevicePostureOverride
commands.description:
Start reporting the given posture value to the Device Posture API.
This override can also be set in setDeviceMetricsOverride().

commands.name: clearDevicePostureOverride
commands.description:
Clears a device posture override set with either setDeviceMetricsOverride()
or setDevicePostureOverride() and starts using posture information from the
platform again.
Does nothing if no override is set.

commands.name: setDisplayFeaturesOverride
commands.description:
Start using the given display features to pupulate the Viewport Segments API.
This override can also be set in setDeviceMetricsOverride().

commands.name: clearDisplayFeaturesOverride
commands.description:
Clears the display features override set with either setDeviceMetricsOverride()
or setDisplayFeaturesOverride() and starts using display features from the
platform again.
Does nothing if no override is set.

parameters.name: hidden
parameters.description: Whether scrollbars should be always hidden.

parameters.name: disabled
parameters.description: Whether document.coookie API should be disabled.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Whether touch emulation based on mouse input should be enabled.

parameters.name: configuration
parameters.description: Touch/gesture events configuration. Default: current platform.

commands.name: setEmulatedMedia
commands.description: Emulates the given media type or media feature for CSS media queries.

parameters.name: media
parameters.description: Media type to emulate. Empty string disables the override.

parameters.name: features
parameters.description: Media features to emulate.

commands.name: setEmulatedVisionDeficiency
commands.description: Emulates the given vision deficiency.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description:
Vision deficiency to emulate. Order: best-effort emulations come first, followed by any
physiologically accurate emulations for medically recognized color vision deficiencies.

commands.name: setEmulatedOSTextScale
commands.description: Emulates the given OS text scale.

commands.name: setGeolocationOverride
commands.description:
Overrides the Geolocation Position or Error. Omitting latitude, longitude or
accuracy emulates position unavailable.

parameters.name: latitude
parameters.description: Mock latitude

parameters.name: longitude
parameters.description: Mock longitude

parameters.name: accuracy
parameters.description: Mock accuracy

parameters.name: altitude
parameters.description: Mock altitude

parameters.name: altitudeAccuracy
parameters.description: Mock altitudeAccuracy

parameters.name: heading
parameters.description: Mock heading

parameters.name: speed
parameters.description: Mock speed

commands.name: setSensorOverrideEnabled
commands.description:
Overrides a platform sensor of a given type. If |enabled| is true, calls to
Sensor.start() will use a virtual sensor as backend rather than fetching
data from a real hardware sensor. Otherwise, existing virtual
sensor-backend Sensor objects will fire an error event and new calls to
Sensor.start() will attempt to use a real sensor instead.

commands.name: setSensorOverrideReadings
commands.description:
Updates the sensor readings reported by a sensor type previously overridden
by setSensorOverrideEnabled.

commands.name: setPressureSourceOverrideEnabled
commands.description:
Overrides a pressure source of a given type, as used by the Compute
Pressure API, so that updates to PressureObserver.observe() are provided
via setPressureStateOverride instead of being retrieved from
platform-provided telemetry data.

commands.name: setPressureStateOverride
commands.description:
TODO: OBSOLETE: To remove when setPressureDataOverride is merged.
Provides a given pressure state that will be processed and eventually be
delivered to PressureObserver users. |source| must have been previously
overridden by setPressureSourceOverrideEnabled.

commands.name: setPressureDataOverride
commands.description:
Provides a given pressure data set that will be processed and eventually be
delivered to PressureObserver users. |source| must have been previously
overridden by setPressureSourceOverrideEnabled.

commands.name: setIdleOverride
commands.description: Overrides the Idle state.

parameters.name: isUserActive
parameters.description: Mock isUserActive

parameters.name: isScreenUnlocked
parameters.description: Mock isScreenUnlocked

commands.name: clearIdleOverride
commands.description: Clears Idle state overrides.

commands.name: setNavigatorOverrides
commands.description: Overrides value returned by the javascript navigator object.

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: The platform navigator.platform should return.

commands.name: setPageScaleFactor
commands.description: Sets a specified page scale factor.

parameters.name: pageScaleFactor
parameters.description: Page scale factor.

commands.name: setScriptExecutionDisabled
commands.description: Switches script execution in the page.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Whether script execution should be disabled in the page.

commands.name: setTouchEmulationEnabled
commands.description: Enables touch on platforms which do not support them.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Whether the touch event emulation should be enabled.

parameters.name: maxTouchPoints
parameters.description: Maximum touch points supported. Defaults to one.

commands.name: setVirtualTimePolicy
commands.description:
Turns on virtual time for all frames (replacing real-time with a synthetic time source) and sets
the current virtual time policy.  Note this supersedes any previous time budget.

parameters.name: budget
parameters.description:
If set, after this many virtual milliseconds have elapsed virtual time will be paused and a
virtualTimeBudgetExpired event is sent.

parameters.name: maxVirtualTimeTaskStarvationCount
parameters.description:
If set this specifies the maximum number of tasks that can be run before virtual is forced
forwards to prevent deadlock.

parameters.name: initialVirtualTime
parameters.description: If set, base::Time::Now will be overridden to initially return this value.

returns.name: virtualTimeTicksBase
returns.description: Absolute timestamp at which virtual time was first enabled (up time in milliseconds).

commands.name: setLocaleOverride
commands.description: Overrides default host system locale with the specified one.

parameters.name: locale
parameters.description:
ICU style C locale (e.g. "en_US"). If not specified or empty, disables the override and
restores default host system locale.

commands.name: setTimezoneOverride
commands.description: Overrides default host system timezone with the specified one.

parameters.name: timezoneId
parameters.description:
The timezone identifier. List of supported timezones:
https://source.chromium.org/chromium/chromium/deps/icu.git/+/faee8bc70570192d82d2978a71e2a615788597d1:source/data/misc/metaZones.txt
If empty, disables the override and restores default host system timezone.

commands.name: setVisibleSize
commands.description:
Resizes the frame/viewport of the page. Note that this does not affect the frame's container
(e.g. browser window). Can be used to produce screenshots of the specified size. Not supported
on Android.

parameters.name: width
parameters.description: Frame width (DIP).

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: Frame height (DIP).

parameters.name: imageTypes
parameters.description: Image types to disable.

commands.name: setDataSaverOverride
commands.description: Override the value of navigator.connection.saveData

parameters.name: dataSaverEnabled
parameters.description: Override value. Omitting the parameter disables the override.

parameters.name: hardwareConcurrency
parameters.description: Hardware concurrency to report

commands.name: setUserAgentOverride
commands.description:
Allows overriding user agent with the given string.
`userAgentMetadata` must be set for Client Hint headers to be sent.

parameters.name: userAgent
parameters.description: User agent to use.

parameters.name: acceptLanguage
parameters.description: Browser language to emulate.

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: The platform navigator.platform should return.

parameters.name: userAgentMetadata
parameters.description: To be sent in Sec-CH-UA-* headers and returned in navigator.userAgentData

commands.name: setAutomationOverride
commands.description: Allows overriding the automation flag.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Whether the override should be enabled.

commands.name: setSmallViewportHeightDifferenceOverride
commands.description:
Allows overriding the difference between the small and large viewport sizes, which determine the
value of the `svh` and `lvh` unit, respectively. Only supported for top-level frames.

parameters.name: difference
parameters.description:
This will cause an element of size 100svh to be `difference` pixels smaller than an element
of size 100lvh.

commands.name: getScreenInfos
commands.description:
Returns device's screen configuration. In headful mode, the physical screens configuration is returned,
whereas in headless mode, a virtual headless screen configuration is provided instead.

commands.name: addScreen
commands.description: Add a new screen to the device. Only supported in headless mode.

parameters.name: left
parameters.description: Offset of the left edge of the screen in pixels.

parameters.name: top
parameters.description: Offset of the top edge of the screen in pixels.

parameters.name: width
parameters.description: The width of the screen in pixels.

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: The height of the screen in pixels.

parameters.name: workAreaInsets
parameters.description: Specifies the screen's work area. Default is entire screen.

parameters.name: devicePixelRatio
parameters.description: Specifies the screen's device pixel ratio. Default is 1.

parameters.name: rotation
parameters.description: Specifies the screen's rotation angle. Available values are 0, 90, 180 and 270. Default is 0.

parameters.name: colorDepth
parameters.description: Specifies the screen's color depth in bits. Default is 24.

parameters.name: label
parameters.description: Specifies the descriptive label for the screen. Default is none.

parameters.name: isInternal
parameters.description: Indicates whether the screen is internal to the device or external, attached to the device. Default is false.

commands.name: updateScreen
commands.description: Updates specified screen parameters. Only supported in headless mode.

parameters.name: screenId
parameters.description: Target screen identifier.

parameters.name: left
parameters.description: Offset of the left edge of the screen in pixels.

parameters.name: top
parameters.description: Offset of the top edge of the screen in pixels.

parameters.name: width
parameters.description: The width of the screen in pixels.

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: The height of the screen in pixels.

parameters.name: workAreaInsets
parameters.description: Specifies the screen's work area.

parameters.name: devicePixelRatio
parameters.description: Specifies the screen's device pixel ratio.

parameters.name: rotation
parameters.description: Specifies the screen's rotation angle. Available values are 0, 90, 180 and 270.

parameters.name: colorDepth
parameters.description: Specifies the screen's color depth in bits.

parameters.name: label
parameters.description: Specifies the descriptive label for the screen.

parameters.name: isInternal
parameters.description: Indicates whether the screen is internal to the device or external, attached to the device. Default is false.

commands.name: removeScreen
commands.description: Remove screen from the device. Only supported in headless mode.

commands.name: setPrimaryScreen
commands.description:
Set primary screen. Only supported in headless mode.
Note that this changes the coordinate system origin to the top-left
of the new primary screen, updating the bounds and work areas
of all existing screens accordingly.

events.name: virtualTimeBudgetExpired
events.description: Notification sent after the virtual time budget for the current VirtualTimePolicy has run out.

events.name: screenOrientationLockChanged
events.description:
Fired when a page calls screen.orientation.lock() or screen.orientation.unlock()
while device emulation is enabled. This allows the DevTools frontend to update the
emulated device orientation accordingly.

parameters.name: locked
parameters.description: Whether the screen orientation is currently locked.

parameters.name: orientation
parameters.description: The orientation lock type requested by the page. Only set when locked is true.
